source_context: UK academic support service — CMI assignment writing and tutoring for working professionals (managers, NHS staff, public sector workers) pursuing Chartered Management Institute qualifications at Levels 3 to 7. Monetises through paid assignment writing, tutoring sessions, and resubmission support across all CMI qualification levels.
central_search_intent: Getting expert help to complete, pass, and understand CMI qualification assignments to the required standard — at the correct command verb depth — for working professionals who cannot afford a referral or a failed submission.

inside_core: All five CMI qualification levels — Level 3 (First Line Management), Level 4 (Management and Leadership), Level 5 (Management and Leadership Diploma), Level 6 (Professional Management and Leadership), Level 7 (Strategic Management and Leadership)
All qualification types at each level — Award, Certificate, and Diploma
All CMI assignment output formats — management report, essay, reflective account, case study analysis, action plan, work-based project
All service delivery types — full assignment writing, assignment tutoring, coursework help, resubmission support, homework help, assignment answers, examples
CMI command verb mastery and alignment — Identify, Describe, Explain, Discuss, Analyse, Evaluate, Justify, Critically Evaluate, Critically Analyse, Assess, Develop a Plan
CMI assignment structure — executive summary, title page, contents page, analysis sections, recommendations, bibliography, appendices
CMI assessment criteria and Learning Outcomes — Pass, Merit, Distinction differentiation
Harvard referencing to CMI standards — ManagementDirect, peer-reviewed journals, CMI publications
UK-specific CMI student profiles — NHS Band 7+, public sector managers, commercial managers, military officers, professional services leaders
Access modality variants — online help, cheap/affordable options, outsourced completion
inside_outer: CMI qualification framework and pathway structure — how levels connect, ACMI/MCMI/FCMI membership progression
CMI qualification level comparison and selection guidance (Level 5 vs Level 7, CMI vs ILM, CMI vs MBA)

Management theories and frameworks used in CMI assignments — Tuckman, Gibbs, Kolb, Kotter, Lewin, Maslow, Herzberg, Porter, Belbin, Bass and Avolio, Thomas-Kilmann, ADKAR, Ansoff, Balanced Scorecard, Resource-Based View
CMI reflective practice methodology — Gibbs' Reflective Cycle, Kolb's Experiential Learning Cycle, CPD portfolio practice
NHS management context — Band structure, management qualifications in NHS, workforce leadership
Management report writing methodology — how to write an executive summary, how to write SMART recommendations, how to structure analysis sections
Academic writing skills for managers — translating professional experience into academic language, argument construction
CMI assessment criteria explained — what assessors look for at each level, Pass vs Merit vs Distinction
Harvard referencing guide for CMI students — citing ManagementDirect, journals, government reports
CMI Membership grades explained — ACMI, MCMI, FCMI, CMgr — what qualifications unlock which grades
Is a CMI qualification worth it? — career ROI, employer recognition, sector value
CMI qualification structure explained — credits, units, how the Diploma is built
Strategic leadership theory — relevant to L7 outer context: distributed leadership, authentic leadership, servant leadership, transactional vs transformational
Organisational strategy frameworks — Porter's Five Forces, PESTLE, Ansoff Matrix — educational context for L7 students
outside_border_excluded: ILM (Institute of Leadership and Management) assignment help — different awarding body, different qualification structure, would dilute CMI source context specificity
CIPD assignment help — HR qualifications, completely different professional domain and student type
ACCA, CIMA, CFA, or any financial/accounting qualification assignment help
University degree or MBA dissertation help — different academic context, different student type, different word count and research methodology requirements
Consumer-facing management content (general leadership articles not connected to CMI assessment)
CMI short courses, apprenticeships, or employer programmes not connected to the Award/Certificate/Diploma qualification structure
Generic study skills content with no CMI connection
topical_border_definition.competitor_border_threat: The primary border risk is generic 'assignment help' sites that list CMI among dozens of qualifications. Their site-wide N-grams are diluted across subjects. cmiassignmentsupport.co.uk must maintain CMI-only specificity in all boilerplate, anchor texts, and N-grams to signal a purpose-built source context rather than a general academic writing service. ILM is the most proximate competitor qualification — some students study both, but the content must not mix the two.

topical_coverage_note: 19 pages live (17 service pages + 2 guide pages). Estimated 22/100 against the full topical border including unit-level pages. Target 60+ at 12 months, 80+ at 24 months. Unit-specific pages (80+ units) represent the largest single coverage gap — each unit page captures a distinct query cluster with its own intent and Learning Outcome context.

core_section.main_attribute: CMI assignment completion and academic success — writing, structuring, and passing CMI qualification unit assignments at the correct command verb depth for the student's qualification level
derived_attributes: Qualification level navigation — matching students to the right level-specific service (L3 → L4 → L5 → L6 → L7)
Assignment format mastery — management report, essay, reflective account, case study, action plan
Command verb depth alignment — matching response depth to Pass/Merit/Distinction standard
Service delivery modality — full writing vs tutoring vs resubmission support
Unit-specific subject coverage — 80+ individual unit assignment topics across all five levels
Affordability and access — affordable options, online delivery, outsourced completion variants

tier_2_nodes.topic: Pay Someone to Do My CMI Assignment

tier_3_planned_nodes.description: Unit-specific landing pages — one page per CMI unit. Each page targets a distinct query cluster (e.g. 'CMI 502 assignment help', 'unit 502 developing managing leading teams') with unique Learning Outcome context, command verb requirements, and format guidance. These are the highest-volume coverage expansion opportunity.
priority_sequence: CMI Level 5 — Units 501, 502, 503, 504, 509 (highest search volume, most requested)
CMI Level 7 — Units 701, 702, 704, 705, 708 (highest value, least competition)

node_template.macro_context: Expert CMI assignment help for Unit [NNN] — [Unit Name] — Learning Outcomes, command verb requirements, format guidance, service options
minimum_eav_per_page: Subject matter coverage (key theories and frameworks)

outer_section.purpose: Process topics from the minor attributes of the CMI qualification central entity. These pages improve historical data by attracting informational search traffic from CMI students at research stage. They propagate trust and topical relevance signals to the Core Section via internal links. They do not sell the assignment writing service directly — they demonstrate authority in the CMI qualification subject matter.
main_minor_attributes_covered: CMI assessment methodology (command verbs, marking criteria, format rules)
Academic writing skills for managers (reflective practice, management report writing, referencing)
Management theory reference (frameworks used in CMI assignments)
Professional context (NHS management, public sector leadership)

outer_nodes.attribute_focus: Every CMI command verb — cognitive depth hierarchy, what each verb requires, how they differ across levels, what Pass/Merit/Distinction responses look like for each verb
outer_nodes.macro_context: Complete CMI command verb guide — every verb from Identify to Critically Analyse, what each requires at each level, and how to apply them correctly in assignments
micro_context_targets: Critically Evaluate vs Critically Analyse distinction
outer_nodes.central_entity_connection: CMI qualification assignment — command verb is a primary attribute of the assessment process
outer_nodes.eav_note: Existing content is strong. SVG assets (command verb ladder) are present. Should expand verb-by-verb worked examples — 'a Level 5 Evaluate response on leadership looks like this'.

outer_nodes.topic: Management Theory Reference — Frameworks Used in CMI Assignments
outer_nodes.attribute_focus: The key management theories and frameworks used in CMI assignment responses — Tuckman, Gibbs, Kolb, Kotter, Lewin, Maslow, Herzberg, Belbin, Porter, Bass and Avolio — what each is, which units use them, how to apply them correctly
outer_nodes.macro_context: Management theory and frameworks for CMI assignments — which models appear at each level, what they cover, and how to apply them at Evaluate and Critically Analyse depth
micro_context_targets: how to apply Tuckman in a CMI 502 management report
how to Evaluate Kotter vs Lewin at L5
how to Critically Analyse Bass and Avolio at L7
limitations of each framework (required for Distinction)
outer_nodes.central_entity_connection: CMI qualification assignment — management theory is a content attribute of assignment responses at all levels
outer_nodes.eav_note: This can be structured as a hub page with individual framework sub-pages (e.g. /guides/tuckman-model-cmi-assignments/). Each framework sub-page captures a specific long-tail query cluster.

outer_nodes.topic: CMI Resubmission Guide — What to Do After a Referral
outer_nodes.attribute_focus: What happens when a CMI assignment is referred — how to read assessor feedback, which Learning Outcomes were unmet, how resubmission support works, what changes are required
outer_nodes.macro_context: CMI resubmission guide — what to do after a CMI referral, how to read assessor feedback, and how to restructure or rewrite to meet unmet Learning Outcomes
micro_context_targets: how resubmission support differs from full writing service
most common resubmission reasons by level
outer_nodes.central_entity_connection: CMI qualification assignment — resubmission is a secondary state attribute of the assignment lifecycle
outer_nodes.eav_note: Students post-referral are high-intent and high-urgency. This page captures them when they are actively searching for a fix. Strong conversion potential.

internal_linking_strategy.primary_rule: Every Core node links outward to its parent level hub and to at least one Outer node. Every Outer node links back to at least two Core nodes.
internal_linking_strategy.anchor_text_rule: Anchor texts must use CMI + level or CMI + service type. Never use generic anchors ('click here', 'learn more', 'this page'). The central entity must appear in anchor text as a synonym variant (e.g. 'CMI Level 5 assignment help', 'Level 5 management report support', 'CMI 502 assignment guidance').
contextual_bridge_priority: T1-05 (Level 5) ↔ O1-01 (Command Verbs) — strongest bridge; Level 5 is where Evaluate and Justify are first demanded at full depth
T1-07 (Level 7) ↔ O1-01 (Command Verbs) — Critically Analyse disambiguation is the Level 7 differentiation content
T1-07 (Level 7) ↔ O2-04 (L5 vs L7 comparison) — comparison page drives level-selection traffic to both hubs
T1-05 (Level 5) ↔ O2-06 (NHS Management) — NHS is the primary student segment for Level 5
T2-04 (Report Writing) ↔ O1-02 (Assignment Structure) — report writing help and structure guide are the same student intent at different purchase stages
T2-01 (Tutoring) ↔ O2-08 (Resubmission Guide) — resubmission is the highest-urgency tutoring trigger

recommended_phase_1.focus: Priority Outer Section pages (O2-04, O2-06, O1-01 expansion, O2-01, O2-08) + CMI 501/502 unit pages
recommended_phase_1.rationale: Outer section pages expand topical coverage into informational query clusters and improve historical data from organic research traffic. High-priority unit pages (501, 502) capture the highest-volume long-tail queries immediately.

recommended_phase_2.focus: All Level 5 unit pages (501–525) + Level 7 priority units (701, 702, 704, 705, 708) + remaining Outer Section nodes
recommended_phase_2.rationale: Unit pages are the single largest topical coverage gap. 25 Level 5 unit pages represent 25 distinct query clusters with specific Learning Outcome intent. Completing this tier moves topical coverage score from 22 to approximately 55.

recommended_phase_3.focus: Level 3, 4, 6 unit pages (39 units) + Level 7 remaining units (12) + management theory sub-pages
recommended_phase_3.rationale: Completes the topical border across all five levels. Management theory sub-pages build the outer section into a reference hub that drives informational traffic and propagates authority to unit pages.
